current_public_release_boundary: v3.4.40 release checks pass with release-forward registry refusal summaries: release-forward verifier packets now preserve the v3.4.39 terminal-chain trusted-issuer registry refusal summary in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json and print it in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md for explicit v3.4.39+ targets. The preserved release-forward terminal_chain_refusal_evidence includes case_count=2, exact case IDs (unrecognized_terminal_chain_registry_scope_refused, registry_receipt_contract_mismatch_refused), exact reason codes (scope_not_found, detail_hash_mismatch), all_refused=true, matching terminal-chain/artifact-verification trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_sha256, and all_trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_preserved=true. DRY-RUN-RESULT.md also mirrors the proof-smoke and North Star readiness Release-Forward Report Contract fields, including Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed-summary preservation. Regression coverage proves v3.4.38 targets omit these release-forward summary fields while v3.4.39+ targets require and preserve them. This is release-forward reporting hardening over already-bounded local fixture refusal evidence only: no live trust-registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no production trust registry, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no current-machine governance, no enterprise readiness, no public external attestation, no real non-operator review, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Explicit false proof flags remain preserved: proves_live_registry=false, proves_live_issuer_status=false, proves_key_custody=false, proves_revocation_truth=false, proves_production_trust_registry=false, proves_production_downstream_recognition=false, proves_production_authority=false, proves_current_machine_governance=false, proves_public_external_attestation=false, proves_real_non_operator_review=false, and proves_sovereign_recognition=false. Previous release evidence remains preserved for audit context: v3.4.39 release checks pass with registry refusal summary surfacing: proof-smoke and North Star readiness now expose terminal-chain-local trusted-issuer registry refusal evidence as first-class evaluator summary fields: case_count=2, exact case IDs (unrecognized_terminal_chain_registry_scope_refused, registry_receipt_contract_mismatch_refused), exact reason codes (scope_not_found, detail_hash_mismatch), all_refused=true, and trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_sha256 for both terminal-chain evidence and terminal-chain artifact verification; North Star readiness mirrors the same fields into Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed summaries. This is summary surfacing of already-bounded local fixture refusal evidence only: no live trust-registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no production trust registry, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no current-machine governance, no enterprise readiness, no public external attestation, no real non-operator review, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Explicit false proof flags remain preserved: proves_live_registry=false, proves_live_issuer_status=false, proves_key_custody=false, proves_revocation_truth=false, proves_production_trust_registry=false, proves_production_downstream_recognition=false, proves_production_authority=false, proves_current_machine_governance=false, proves_public_external_attestation=false, proves_real_non_operator_review=false, and proves_sovereign_recognition=false. The previous v3.4.38 release preserved terminal-chain-local trusted-issuer registry refusal evidence inside the hash-bound trusted issuer registry recognition binding: unrecognized registry scope refuses with scope_not_found; a valid signed receipt whose registry-bound receipt contract mismatches refuses with detail_hash_mismatch while preserving signature_valid=true and issuer_status=active; artifact verification and CLI preserve case_count=2, all_refused=true, exact case IDs/reasons, trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_sha256, registry_receipt_contract_hash_bound=true, and the full false-boundary field contract. This remains bundled-local-fixture terminal-chain evidence only: no live trust-registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no production trust registry, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no current-machine governance, no enterprise readiness, no public external attestation, no real non-operator review, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage.  Explicit false proof flags remain preserved: proves_live_registry=false, proves_live_issuer_status=false, proves_key_custody=false, proves_revocation_truth=false, proves_production_trust_registry=false, proves_production_downstream_recognition=false, proves_production_authority=false, proves_current_machine_governance=false, proves_public_external_attestation=false, proves_real_non_operator_review=false, and proves_sovereign_recognition=false. The previous v3.4.37 release bound terminal-chain trusted-issuer registry recognition to the selected installed-runtime profile, preflight, service proof, service-proof artifact verification, terminal-chain artifact, and terminal-chain artifact verification: v3.4.37 local terminal-chain, proof-smoke, and North Star readiness evidence preserve terminal_chain_trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_binding / trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_binding as a public-safe hash-bound registry/receipt summary with registry_contract_sha256, receipt_contract_sha256, registry_receipt_contract_hash_bound=true, registry_public_key_material_included=false, receipt_envelope_included=false, and cryptographic_evidence_reproducible_from_artifact=false; the fixture registry evaluates to RECOGNIZED, malformed registries fail closed before verdict, proof-smoke and North Star readiness preserve the full terminal-chain registry non-claim flags in Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed summaries, and false live-registry, live-issuer-status, key-custody, revocation-truth, production-trust-registry, production-downstream-recognition, production-authority, current-machine-governance, public-attestation, real-non-operator-review, and sovereign-recognition claim flags remain false. The previous v3.4.36 release made the Product Proof Path trusted-issuer registry recognition component first-class while preserving release-forward trusted-issuer registry recognition evidence, private result verification evidence, issuer-status fixture evidence, release-asset readiness handoff, and verifier-owned terminal-chain nested binding evidence: v3.4.36 local proof-pack and Product Proof Path reports include trusted_issuer_registry_recognition as a first-class component, validate and evaluate a valid bundled trusted-receipt-issuers-v1 registry fixture through the shared downstream recognition rule, and record registry_fixture_validated=true, registry_fixture_evaluated=true, registry_to_recognition_rule_evaluated=true, registry_evaluation_result_type=downstream-recognition-rule-v1, registry_trusted_issuer_count=1, required audit-event/detail-hash binding, issuer_status=active, signature_valid=true, and malformed_registry_fail_closed_before_verdict=true; proof-smoke and North Star readiness consume that component instead of reconstructing registry recognition from adjacent summaries; false live-registry, live-issuer-status, key-custody, revocation-truth, production-trust-registry, production-downstream-recognition, production-authority, current-machine-governance, public-attestation, real-non-operator-review, and sovereign-recognition claim flags remain false. The previous v3.4.35 release made release-forward trusted-issuer registry recognition evidence first-class: v3.4.35 release-forward verifier packets include trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_evidence in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json and a Trusted Issuer Registry Recognition Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, binding the supplied recognition JSON, malformed-registry JSON, and malformed-registry error hashes; recording registry_type=trusted-receipt-issuers-v1, registry_evidence_model=bundled-local-fixture, live_probing=false, verdict=RECOGNIZED, issuer_status=active, signature_valid=true, and malformed_registry_fail_closed_before_verdict=true; and preserving false live-registry, live-issuer-status, key-custody, revocation-truth, production-trust-registry, production-downstream-recognition, production-authority, sovereign-recognition, public-attestation, public-attribution, and non-operator-review claim flags. The previous v3.4.34 release made private result verification evidence first-class: v3.4.34 release-forward verifier packets include a Private Result Verification Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, preserving private-result verification verdict, recomputed evidence-dir hash status, artifact count, sample/non-operator boundary, private_non_operator_pass_validated=false for generated sample evidence, and false public-attestation/public-attribution/public-non-operator-review claim flags. This is bundled-local-fixture and terminal-chain registry recognition reviewability over generated local/release-forward evidence. It does not prove live registry state, live issuer status, key custody, revocation truth, production trust registry, production downstream recognition, production authority, sovereign recognition, public external attestation, real non-operator review, or enterprise readiness. v3.4.33 continues to provide issuer-status evidence reviewability: release-forward verifier packets include issuer_status_evidence in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json and an Issuer Status Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, preserving the local hermetic issuer-status proof, verifier-kit issuer fixture verdict, active-issuer boarding, retired/compromised/missing-status/unknown/missing-key issuer refusals, no-live boundary, and false live/custody/revocation/production-registry/downstream claim flags. v3.4.32 continues the release-asset readiness handoff without widening asset claims: release-forward verifier dry runs use live explicit-tag GitHub release-asset reads plus --require-public public-distribution validation only for the explicit asset-bearing v3.4.31 public ZLAR-AI/ZLAR verifier-kit target; later proof-hardening releases do not inherit a public asset claim unless that exact release publishes the required assets, and North Star readiness rebuilds public-distribution posture from raw release-asset, reproducibility, and local dist-file evidence before accepting a public-ready claim. v3.4.31 remains the uploaded byte-bound verifier-kit asset evidence: live public release assets for the verifier kit are published, zlar verifier-kit-release-assets-live-read emits zlar-verifier-kit-release-assets-live-v1 with all required assets present and downloaded, and zlar verifier-kit-public-distribution --require-public reports public_distribution_posture_ready with ready_for_public_distribution_claim=true. v3.4.30 proof-smoke, North Star readiness, and release-forward evidence preserve terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_binding as a verifier-owned artifact-verification summary with nested artifact body hashes, verified flags, binding booleans, and false stronger-claim flags. v3.4.28+ terminal-chain reports and portable artifacts embed nested generated preflight and service-proof artifacts, and release-forward dry-run exports terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_tamper_refusals with forged_inner_preflight_hash_refused=true and forged_inner_service_hash_refused=true after refusing forged inner preflight and service-proof hashes while recomputing outer terminal-chain artifact integrity. Prior readiness evidence remains preserved: v3.4.25+ proof-smoke and North Star readiness preserve group_count=3, case_count=18, and exact grouped case IDs for both terminal-chain evidence and terminal-chain artifact verification. For v3.4.26+ the Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed summaries mirror that same case-ID contract. v3.4.24+ release-forward verifier packets still preserve terminal_chain_refusal_evidence, recognition_refusal_group_case_ids, recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_required=true, and all_recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_preserved=true for no usable recognized receipt authority, recognized receipt scope mismatch, and route/request authority material refusal groups. v3.4.23+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve recognition_refusal_groups_sha256 and grouped before-mutation booleans. v3.4.22+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve named_receipt_refusals_sha256 and named refusal booleans for missing, invalid, stale, unknown-issuer, wrong-policy, wrong-domain, and wrong-tool receipt cases. v3.4.21+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve zlar-verifier-kit-external-runner-diagnostics-v1.json, and v3.4.19+ readiness, proof-smoke, and release-forward dry-run checks still preserve installed-runtime-profile recognition-contract SHA-256 binding. Current release title: Release-forward registry refusal summaries. Manifest field inventory preserved for verifier tooling: zlar-trusted-receipt-issuer-recognition-malformed-registry.json, zlar-trusted-receipt-issuer-recognition-malformed-registry-error.txt, nested_artifacts, terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_tamper_refusals.forged_inner_preflight_hash_refused, terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_tamper_refusals.forged_inner_service_hash_refused, v3.4.26+ North Star readiness observed summaries mirror the terminal-chain case-ID contract, installed_runtime_profile_recognition_contract_sha256, and all_trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_preserved=true. This remains local disposable installed-runtime-profile recognition-refusal group case-id, recognition-refusal group, and named-refusal evidence only.
claim_ceiling: ZLAR can govern Codex CLI-invoked MCP tool calls when those MCP servers are routed through ZLAR.

routed_mcp_proof_report.reason: referenced by hash and sanitized call summary only

non_claims: This report covers routed or intercepted action surfaces only.
This report does not assert coverage for Codex shell, filesystem, browser, app-control, direct network, model reasoning, or final text surfaces.
MCP servers registered directly with a client instead of through the ZLAR MCP gate are outside this report.
Public external verifier attestation is not claimed in v0.
residual_ungoverned_surfaces: Codex shell commands outside routed MCP tools/call decisions
Codex filesystem changes outside routed MCP tools/call decisions
Codex browser actions outside routed MCP tools/call decisions
Codex desktop app-control actions outside routed MCP tools/call decisions
Codex direct network calls outside routed MCP tools/call decisions
Codex model reasoning and final text
MCP protocol messages other than tools/call decisions
MCP servers registered directly with the client instead of through the ZLAR MCP gate
